rxn_class_summary: Electrochemically initiated catalytic dithiolation (addition of ArSSAr) to alkenes and alkynes via a cationic chain mechanism mediated by an arylbis(arylthio)sulfonium ion ArS(ArSSAr)+.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ArSSAr at -78 °C generates and accumulates a catalytic 'cation pool' of ArS(ArSSAr)+ (1), which then initiates a non-electrochemical cation chain with the alkene and additional ArSSAr.

key_intermediates.name: arylbis(arylthio)sulfonium ion ArS(ArSSAr)+ (1), chain carrier / initiator
thiiranium ion A from ArS+ transfer to alkene
β-arylthio sulfonium ion B (open-chain sulfonium after ArSSAr attack on A)

electron_flow: Anode removes one electron from ArSSAr to build up a catalytic pool of ArS(ArSSAr)+ (1); 1 transfers ArS+ to the alkene forming thiiranium A, which is opened by another ArSSAr to give sulfonium B, which fragments to dithiolated product C and regenerates 1, propagating a cation chain without further electron transfer.

probe_reagents.name: Bu4NB(C6F5)4 vs Bu4NBF4 (counteranion probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Yields drop sharply with BF4- (≤26%) vs B(C6F5)4- (71-79%); less nucleophilic/less stabilizing B(C6F5)4- keeps cations A and B electrophilic enough for ArSSAr attack, supporting cationic chain.

probe_reagents.name: Catalytic electricity (0.06 F/mol) experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Substoichiometric charge (ca. 6 mol% of 1 generated) still drives full conversion, consistent with a chain process with 1 as chain carrier.

probe_reagents.name: Et3N quench
probe_reagents.observation: Added to quench cationic intermediates and terminate the reaction.

notes: Mechanism builds on prior stoichiometric ArS(ArSSAr)+ 'cation pool' chemistry (thiiranium intermediates trapped by hard/soft nucleophiles). No radical traps, KIE, CV, or DFT reported on these pages; mechanistic support comes from catalytic-charge experiment, counteranion effect, and anti-selective addition consistent with thiiranium opening. Alternative pathways (e.g., radical chain) are not explicitly excluded.
